qa_pairs.question: How do you submit an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job using Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) on an Alliance compute cluster with CPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit an Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) job using Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) with CPUs, you would typically use a Slurm script. This script should load `StdEnv/2023` and an Ansys module like `ansys/2024R1.03` (or newer). The core command to run the simulation is `mapdl -smp -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`, where `$SLURM_NTASKS` specifies the number of cores requested via `#SBATCH --tasks`. An output directory named `outdir-$SLURM_JOBID` will be created for the job.

qa_pairs.question: What specific environment variable modification is necessary for running Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) jobs on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: For Ansys Mechanical (MAPDL) jobs on the Cedar cluster, the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` environment variable needs to be set. This is achieved by including `export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$EBROOTGCC/../lib/gcc` in your Slurm script, specifically when the `CC_CLUSTER` variable is `cedar`.
